blockers.title: [CLAIM] tag overclaims OTCP marginal coverage vs the test it cites
blockers.claim_text: [CLAIM] OTCP split conformal ... achieves marginal coverage >= 1 - alpha on synthetic exchangeable data over 100 bootstrap trials, 95% of the time (tests/test_otcp.py).
blockers.test_actual: n_trials = 60 (line 60), alpha = 0.2 fixed (line 58), 15% coverage slack `coverage >= target_coverage - 0.15` (line 78), threshold `hits / n_trials >= 0.5` (line 80). Therefore test verifies: 60 trials, 50% pass rate, within 15% of target at a single alpha.
blockers.delta: CLAIM says 100/95%; test enforces 60/50% with 15% slack. The [CLAIM] tag is unbacked by its own cited test. Honest-marketing absolutism violated.
fix_options: (a) raise n_trials to 100, threshold to >= 0.95, drop the 15% slack, sweep alpha — then keep CLAIM text.

(b) soften CLAIM.md:9 to mirror the actual test: 'achieves marginal coverage within 15% of 1 - alpha at alpha=0.2 over 60 bootstrap trials, at least 50% of the time (tests/test_otcp.py)' — and also update the test docstring line 55 to stop saying 100/95%.

majors.title: CLAIM.md tag placement does not satisfy HONEST-PREALPHA-COMPLETE §7 spec grep
majors.evidence_file: docs/CLAIM.md (all bullet lines) vs bootstrap_protocol §7 line 623
majors.spec_grep: grep -E '^\[(CLAIM|non-CLAIM)\]' docs/CLAIM.md  # protocol §7 says this MUST match every relevant line
majors.actual: Every CLAIM line starts with `- ` (bullet) then `[CLAIM]` / `[non-CLAIM]` — so the §7 line-leading grep returns 0 matches.
majors.ci_yml_workaround: audit.yml uses a different grep (`grep -E '^- ' | grep -vE '\[(CLAIM|non-CLAIM)\]'`) which DOES pass — so CI green, but S11 §7 verification will fail.
fix_options: (a) update bootstrap_protocol §7 line 623 to match the audit.yml grep (RECOMMENDED — audit.yml is more robust for bullet style).
(b) reformat docs/CLAIM.md so every CLAIM line starts at column 0 with `[CLAIM]` / `[non-CLAIM]` (sacrifices markdown bullet rendering).
majors.consensus_with_C: Agent C flagged the same issue as C-B3 — independent confirmation.

majors.title: PAVA 'strict copy + inherits foldconsensus tests' CLAIM is unverified by tests/test_calibration.py
majors.claim_text: [CLAIM] PAVA monotone isotonic regression (vendored from foldconsensus, Apache-2.0) is a strict copy and inherits foldconsensus's PAVA tests.
majors.test_actual: tests/test_calibration.py contains 7 freshly-written PAVA tests (test_pava_already_monotone_is_identity, test_pava_strict_decreasing_becomes_constant_mean, etc.) that look like newly authored invariant checks, not literal copies of upstream foldconsensus tests. No header attribution 'copied from foldconsensus/tests/...' is present.
majors.fix: Soften CLAIM.md:11 to: '[CLAIM] PAVA monotone isotonic regression is vendored from foldconsensus (Apache-2.0 with attribution in src/polyalign/calibration/pava.py header) and re-verified by polyalign-side property tests in tests/test_calibration.py.'

majors.title: scripts/verify_step.py missing — /compact resume path will cold-restart from S0
majors.evidence_file: scripts/ (no verify_step.py) vs bootstrap_protocol §1 S1 line 99 + §3 line 494
majors.spec: S1 explicitly lists 'scripts/verify_step.py (各 phase 機械検証)' as a required scaffold artifact; §3 line 494: 'verify_step.py 不在 → current_step = "S0" で cold-build'.
majors.impact: S8/S9/S10 self-resume after /compact will fall back to S0 cold-build, losing protocol state. Not a SHIP-quality blocker for the user-facing v0.1.0a1 artifact, but a protocol-fidelity blocker for the self-drive contract.

nits.title: README:75 uses idiom 'first-class SSM carrier support' — borderline on checklist 2 ('first' self-claim absence)
nits.note: 'first-class' is a standard idiom for 'native / fully-supported'; banned-phrases.txt only blocks 'first ever', so CI green. But Agent B checklist item 2 reads 'first / 世界初 自称が ... 不在'. Recommend rephrase to 'native SSM carrier support' to remove ambiguity entirely.
nits.ci_impact: none (CI passes); pure cosmetic for honest-marketing strictness.

nits.title: neelnanda Crosscoders missing from README prior-art table (checklist 3 lists 6 competitors; README names 5)
nits.fix: Add one bullet: '**neelnanda Crosscoders** (interpretability tutorials) — pedagogical 2-model crosscoder. polyalign is N>=2 multi-architecture with OTCP coverage band per vertex.'
nits.ci_impact: none (CI grep only requires gavagai/AlignSAE/crosscoder — all present).

nits.title: [project.scripts] entry-point name drift vs protocol
nits.note: Live `uv run polyalign-lint --help` works (verified rc=0); entry resolves. Protocol intent satisfied — but text drift between protocol and impl. Pick one and align.

nits.title: Word 'novel' used 3x in README/CHANGELOG for polytope-vertex core
nits.note: 'novel' is softer than 'first' but is a self-claim of originality. The composition (Pareto-front + cycle-consistency on top of pairwise Sinkhorn-OT alignments) is plausibly novel given the prior-art table, so defensible. Suggest 'polyalign-specific' or 'newly composed' if maximum honest-marketing strictness preferred.

checklist_results.1_banned_phrase_grep: pass — banned-phrases.txt ERE OR-join over README/docs/NOTICE/CHANGELOG/SECURITY returns rc=1 (no match); printf-fed grep returns rc=0 as expected (mechanics verified).
checklist_results.2_first_world_first_self_claim: pass-with-nit — no 'first ever' / '世界初' / '業界初' anywhere; 'first-class SSM carrier support' is idiomatic but borderline (B-N1).
checklist_results.3_prior_art_diff: pass-with-nit — gavagai/AlignSAE/Anthropic crosscoder/ckkissane/SPARC + OpenMOSS named in README:70-76; neelnanda missing per checklist 3 (B-N2).
checklist_results.4_CLAIM_consistency: FAIL — CLAIM.md:9 [CLAIM] OTCP 100/95% but test_otcp.py implements 60/50% with 15% slack (B-B1).
checklist_results.5_synthetic_DEMO_disclaimer: pass — '[DEMO]' grep over README (3 hits) / CLAIM.md (2 hits) / CHANGELOG.md (1 hit) all present; 36/36 ground-truth rows carry [DEMO]; ablation env_stamp.mode='synthetic' + case='C (S0 OQ3 degrade)'.
checklist_results.6_project_scripts_entry_real: pass — `uv run polyalign-lint --help` rc=0, lists version/smoke/align subcommands; entry resolves to polyalign.cli:app (NIT B-N3 on name drift vs protocol).
checklist_results.7_vacuous_test_detection: pass — `grep -rE '^\s*assert\s+True\s*$' tests/` rc=1 (no match). test_polytope.py:16 has `assert 1 <= len(verts) <= 3` which is a real range bound, not vacuous.
checklist_results.8_placeholder_grep: pass — no random literals or unjustified 4-digit float constants in src/polyalign/; all README floats reproducible byte-identical from results/v0.1.0a1_ablation.json (`uv run python scripts/render_readme.py && git diff --exit-code README.md` rc=0); CLI line 35 `np.random.default_rng(0)` is seeded smoke-test, not placeholder.

actual: alpha=0.2 fixed, n_trials=60, 15% slack, threshold hits/n_trials >= 0.5

top1=top5=0.000 is structurally expected under Case C (random Gaussian decoders + arbitrary synthetic labels); README:124 and CLAIM.md:25 honestly disclose this — Agent B does NOT concur with Agent C's C-B2 diagnosis of 'likely logic bug'. The zeros are an artifact of the honest synthetic setup, not a bug.
`assert 1 <= len(verts) <= 3` — real range check, not vacuous
36/36 rows carry [DEMO] prefix in prompt + concept_label; source_url=synthetic://polyalign/v0.1.0a1/Case-C — full disclosure

prior_art_diff_table.polyalign_diff: pairwise scalar score → polyalign returns ranked top-k vertex set with coverage band + Pareto front (N >= 2, M >= 1)
training-time concept-aligned SAEs → polyalign is post-hoc over pre-trained SAEs (no retraining)
closed 2-model diff → polyalign open-source N-model multi-architecture with OTCP coverage
Euclidean 2-model replication → polyalign adds OTCP + Sinkhorn-OT + SSM carrier
training-time concept-aligned SAEs cross-modal → polyalign post-hoc, no SAE retraining
pedagogical 2-model crosscoder → polyalign N >= 2 multi-architecture with OTCP coverage band — NIT B-N2: should be added to README:70-76
per-model Matryoshka SAE → polyalign cross-architecture Matryoshka with Sinkhorn-OT (extra mention in README:76)

agent_C.C-B1 (A+B verdicts absent): now resolved — Agent B writing concurrently with Agent C run; A still pending separately.
agent_C.C-B2 (top1/top5 = 0.000 'likely bug'): DISAGREE — Agent B reads run_ablation.py:95-109 (modulo on n_feat) + random Gaussian decoders + arbitrary synthetic labels and concludes the zeros are structurally expected under Case C and are honestly disclosed at README:124. Agent B does NOT treat C-B2 as a ship blocker. Recommend Agent C re-classify as nit or withdraw.

verdict_text: DO-NOT-SHIP. One CRITICAL blocker (B-B1): docs/CLAIM.md:9 [CLAIM] tag asserts OTCP coverage at 100 trials / 95% rate, but tests/test_otcp.py implements 60 trials / 50% rate with 15% slack at fixed alpha=0.2 — the [CLAIM] is unverified by its cited test, violating honest-marketing absolutism. Three MAJOR issues (CLAIM tag format vs §7 grep, PAVA 'inherits tests' overclaim, missing verify_step.py for /compact resume). Four NITs. Agent B agrees with Agent C on the CLAIM-format issue but DISAGREES that top1=top5=0.000 is a logic bug — those zeros are the honestly disclosed outcome of random Gaussian decoders against arbitrary synthetic labels under Case C. Fix B-B1 by either raising the test or softening the CLAIM, and rerun S8 critic gate.
